subject: Cloud Computing
title: CQRS: Reads vs. Writes
description: A micro-lesson that makes CQRS: Reads vs. Writes usable.

hook.text: Your app reads data 100x more than it writes. Why use the same model for both? CQRS says: don't.

buildup.text: Command Query Responsibility Segregation uses separate models for reading and writing data.

discovery.text: The write side handles commands and business logic. The read side serves optimized, denormalized views.

twist.text: CQRS adds complexity. Eventually consistent reads mean the read side might be briefly out of date after a write.

climax.text: For high-read, low-write systems, CQRS delivers massive performance gains. For simple apps, it's overkill.

punchline.text: Separate reads from writes. Each can be optimized independently.

quiz.question: What is the core idea behind CQRS?
options: Using separate optimized models for reading and writing data
Running all database queries in a single thread
Storing data only in memory, never on disk
